message:
fix(guard,scrub): fail-closed reservation release check and preserve deep JSON on recursion limit

guard/lib.rs — Fix released_ts_marks_record_released() Python-evaluated
logic to fail closed on ambiguous inputs:
- Unparseable numeric strings (ValueError in float()) now return False
  instead of True, preventing non-numeric released_ts values from being
  treated as "released"
- Unknown JSON types (not String, Number, or Null) now return False
  instead of True, preventing unexpected schema extensions from silently
  releasing reservations
These were fail-open bugs: any reservation with a malformed released_ts
would be treated as released, allowing conflicting reservations to be
granted.

scrub.rs — Fix scrub_structure() recursion depth guard: when the depth
limit (256) is exceeded, return value.clone() instead of Value::Null.
The previous behavior silently replaced deeply nested JSON with null,
which is data corruption when scrubbing legitimate large attachment
payloads. The depth limit exists to prevent stack overflow on malicious
inputs, not to truncate valid data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
